After the Romanovs were executed, rumours claimed Anastasia survived. Anna Anderson later emerged as a claimant. Narrated by Robert Powell.
This unique series narrated by Sir Tony Robinson uses home movie archive footage to reveal life in 20th-century Britain as never seen before - this episode features footage from the 1930s. S1 E1
How close did the superpowers actually come to a nuclear holocaust? We reveal new information on the brutal power struggles behind the Iron Curtain. S2 E6: Cold War - The Strangelove Factor
The Ju 52 is a German transport aircraft that delivered the attacking forces and their supplies during the German invasion of Norway, Denmark, France and the Low Countries in WWII. S3 E2

programmes.show_name: Nazi Quest for the Holy Grail
programmes.show_description: In the final days of WWII, American troops discovered a hoard of mysterious Nazi files hidden in a cave; these secret documents were the details of one of the Third Reich's most shadowy projects.
Despite the general inexperience of its young pilots, the Royal Air Force Fighter Command ultimately prevailed in its battle against the German Luftwaffe. Ep6: RAF Fighter Command
The course of the fight by the British and American navies against the German submarine U-Boat threat - a tense cat-and-mouse struggle that by the end of the war had cost over 100,000 lives.

Britain faced Nazi Germany in July 1940 as Winston Churchill set up the Commandos, a fighting force to raid enemy coasts, and ordered a major assault on Vaagso in Norway. Ep6: Arctic Commando Assaults
At the outbreak of WWII, the only heavy four-engined bombers in the war were either Russian or American - soon the Short Stirling, the Avro Lancaster, and the Liberator were added. Ep6: Heavy Bombers
We hear first-hand accounts of the incredible sacrifices and momentous feats endured by the Allies alongside the devastating consequences of the German capture of France. Ep1: Fall of France
Battles are fought in WWII in every continent in many different landscapes; the British are cut off at Dunkirk after the unstoppable Nazis tear through western Europe. Ep3: Flexible Strategies
How the capture of the Germans' Cipher codebreaking machine permitted one of the greatest campaigns of deception in military history. E2: The Ultra Enigma
It's June 1909 as Elizabeth marries Lawrence Kirbridge, a poet and Fabian socialist; her parents have their doubts, but they welcome him to the family. S1 E13: For the Love of Love
Arthur attempts to do a deal with Tony Benson and his new nightclub. Terry helps Dave by looking after the Winchester Club. S7 E2: Days of Fines and Closures
Prostitution and the killing of a colleague put Dempsey and Makepeace on the trail of a professional assassin dedicated to killing a top official. S2 E8: The Hit
Alex believes that she can leave the prison of 1981 and get back home to her daughter Molly if she can go back to the day her parents were killed and prevent their death. S1 E8
With Else off in Australia, Alf has a serious problem. Who will cook his dinner when he gets back from work? S6 E2: Phone Call to Else

The Red Baron, Germany's top WWI ace, was shot down in 1918. Though he landed his plane, he died soon after but was it accidental? Narrated by Robert Powell.
This unique series narrated by Sir Tony Robinson uses home movie archive footage to reveal life in 20th-century Britain as never seen before - this episode features footage from the 1940s. S1 E2
Discover the secret telegrams that reveal how Stalin had a personal role in air battles with UN pilots and how Britain unwillingly aided communists. S2 E7: Korea - Stalin's Secret Air War
The Republic P-47 Thunderbolt is a WWII-era fighter aircraft. It was a successful high-altitude fighter, and it also served as the foremost American fighter-bomber in the ground-attack role. S3 E3

programmes.show_name: Margaret Thatcher: The Lady's Not for Turning
programmes.show_description: We examine the life and career of Margaret Thatcher, Britain's first female Prime Minister and one of the dominant political figures of 20th-century Britain.
One of the world's elite WWII fighting forces, the Commandos were created by Churchill in the summer of 1940 as a means of striking at the coasts of Nazi-occupied Europe. Ep7: The Paras and Commandos
Operation Market Garden was an attempt by the allies to use airborne units to seize key bridges and short cut the attack into northern Germany. We look at the plan, leaders and the weapons involved.
The story of the boffins and experimenters who developed the first workable radar system in time to help defeat the Nazi attacks in the Battle of Britain. Ep1: The Men Who Invented Radar
Battleships were seen as the key weapon at sea at the beginning of World War II, but their vulnerability was exposed by submarines and aircraft over the next six years. Ep7: The Battleship
The battle progresses from the ground to the sky as the British begin to face the Germans over the buildings of London and other British towns. Ep2: Battle of Britain
Battles are almost always about gaining territory; soldiers in the bombed remains of Stalingrad are sentenced to death or held captive. Ep4: Seizing Territory
From undercover agents in the French Resistance to women who were willing to get under the covers in exchange for military secrets, discover the secret world of women at war. E3: Women Spies of WW2
Mr and Mrs Kirbridge return from their honeymoon in Austria and set up their new household in nearby Greenwich, but are the couple as happy as they should be? S2 E1: The New Man
A gambler dies owing Arthur Â£2000, but Terry has other problems to contend with when an old flame turns up. With guest star Billy Connolly. S7 E3: Fatal Impression
Dempsey and Makepeace are put on the case when a colleague's death brings his investigation into a group of right-wing extremists to light. S2 E9: In The Dark
The team must venture into dangerous territory when a fellow officer is found dead, and Alex's whole perspective on the world of 1981 is threatened when she is kidnapped. S2 E1
